title: Use digest to pin versions of base images
description: Use digest to pin versions of base imagesAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityreliabilitysecurityCode SmellIn Dockerfiles, it is recommended to use digests to pin versions of base images. This practice ensures that you are always using the exact version of the base image that you intend to use, making your Docker image builds reproducible.Why is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoIf you’re using tags to specify the version of your base image, you might not get the same image every time. Tags are mutable, meaning they can be updated to point to a different image.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Use digest to pin versions of base images. Image tags are mutable and can be updated to point to different images, making builds non-reproducible. Pin the image version using its SHA256 digest (e.g., 'FROM ubuntu:20.04@sha256:abc123...') to ensure you always get the exact same image and guarantee reproducible builds.
